claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 15 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 15 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child is not in local authority care
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 15 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child is not in local authority care; Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child is not in local authority care
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine if you're eligible for Child Benefit. To assess your eligibility, I'll need to ask you some questions.

First, let me read the official guidance to ensure I provide you with accurate information.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for waiting. Now let me ask you some questions to determine your eligibility.

Do you have any children that you're responsible for?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I do. I have one child – my son Alex. He's 15 years old and lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. It's helpful to know that you have Alex, who is 15 years old and lives with you.

Do you live in the UK?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK – I'm in Manchester.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
That's great, thank you. 

Is Alex currently in approved education or training?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Well, he's only 15, so he's still in school. He hasn't left yet – he's doing his GCSEs at the moment.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's very helpful.

Just to confirm - is anyone else currently claiming Child Benefit for Alex, or has anyone else made a claim for him?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nobody else is claiming Child Benefit for him. It's just me looking after him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Perfect, thank you. Just a few more questions to complete the assessment.

Has Alex been in hospital or care for an extended period (more than 8-12 weeks), or is there any arrangement where your local council is paying towards his accommodation or maintenance?

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: No, nothing like that. He's just been living at home with me the whole time. He's not been in hospital or care, and the council isn't paying anything towards him.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for providing all that information. I now have everything I need to assess your eligibility for Child Benefit for Alex.

Based on what you've told me, let me provide you with my assessment:

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
**Good news! You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

You meet all the eligibility criteria:
- Alex is 15 years old (under 16)
- He lives with you in the UK
- You are responsible for him
- He is in approved education (attending school)
- No one else is claiming Child Benefit for him

You can apply for Child Benefit and should be entitled to receive it. Please note that if you or your partner have an individual income over £60,000, you may need to pay the High Income Child Benefit tax charge, but you would still be eligible to claim.

Is there anything else you'd like to know about Child Benefit?

reasonings: Alex is 15 years old (under 16), lives with you in the UK, and is in approved education (attending school doing GCSEs). You are responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. There are no special circumstances such as hospital stays, care arrangements, or local council payments that would affect eligibility. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.
arguments.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 15), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. Alex is in approved education (school, doing GCSEs). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances affecting eligibility. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.

You are responsible for him, and no one else is claiming Child Benefit for him. There are no special circumstances such as hospital stays, care arrangements, or local council payments that would affect eligibility. According to the eligibility rules, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK, and you'll usually be responsible for a child if you live with them. All these conditions are met for Alex.

response.overall_reasoning: The claimant is responsible for one child, Alex (age 15), who lives with them in Manchester, UK. Alex is in approved education (school, doing GCSEs). No one else is claiming Child Benefit for Alex, and there are no special circumstances affecting eligibility. The claimant meets all the standard eligibility criteria for Child Benefit.
